file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object class for HP Smart Windows app Mobile Fax feature automation. Contains MobileFax which inherits HPSmartBasicActions and provides all Appium tuple locators for Mobile Fax compose screen, recipient fields, sender fields, file attachment, cover page, scan-to-fax, fax settings, contacts management, and cover page templates. Provides complete workflow methods for composing faxes, validating fax delivery status, managing drafts, uploading files, scanning to fax, saving contacts, and creating cover page templates.

classes.class_purpose: Page object class for automating the HP Smart Windows app Mobile Fax feature using Appium WinAppDriver. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions which provides base Appium interaction methods. Defines class-level Appium tuple locators for all Mobile Fax UI controls including compose fax fields (To, From, Files & Cover Page tabs), recipient/sender input fields, file attachment buttons, scan-to-fax controls, fax settings, contacts management, and cover page template creation. Provides complete fax workflow methods: navigate to Mobile Fax, enter recipient/sender info, attach files or scan documents, configure cover pages, send fax, validate delivery status (Dialing → Ringing → Sending → Fax Delivered), manage drafts, save contacts, and create cover page templates.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance from appium_driver pytest fixture for HP Smart Windows app automation

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the MobileFax page object by calling the parent HPSmartBasicActions constructor with super().__init__(driver) and storing the driver reference as self.driver. All locators are defined as class-level attributes outside __init__ so no locator setup happens in the constructor. This class requires an active Appium WinAppDriver session passed via the appium_driver pytest fixture.

Navigates from HP Smart app home screen to the Mobile Fax compose screen. First waits for printer_online status using wait_for_object() with basic_wait_time timeout. Checks if mobile_fax tile is displayed using element_displayed(), clicks it using click_me(), then checks if compose_fax button is displayed and clicks it. If compose_fax screen loads successfully, checks if clear_all_fields button is enabled using wait_for_element_and_check_enabled() and clicks it to reset all fields to empty state. Logs each step using logging.info(). Asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

parameters.name: fax_num
parameters.description: Recipient fax number to enter. Defaults to class constant fax_number_to which is '2135312487'

parameters.name: to_name_
parameters.description: Recipient display name to enter. Defaults to 'Recepient'

functions.what_it_does: Enters recipient fax number and name in the Mobile Fax To tab. Sends fax_num to the fax_number input field using send_keys_to_element(). Checks if to_name_field is displayed with 10 second timeout, sends to_name_ to it, and logs the entered name. Then checks if from_link_ID is displayed with 20 second timeout and clicks it to navigate to the From tab. Logs each step and asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

parameters.name: phone_num
parameters.description: Sender phone number to enter. Defaults to class constant from_phone_number which is '8558250000'

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Sender name to enter. Defaults to 'fax_01'

functions.what_it_does: Enters sender phone number in the Mobile Fax From tab. Sends phone_num to the phone_number input field using send_keys_to_element(). Note that the from_name field send is commented out in the code. Then checks if files_and_cover_page_ID is displayed with 10 second timeout and clicks it to navigate to the Files and Cover Page tab. Logs each step and asserts True/False based on element visibility.

Toggles the Add a Cover Page feature from Off to On in the Files and Cover Page tab. Checks if add_cover_off toggle button is displayed, clicks it to turn the cover page feature on, logs the action, and asserts True. If add_cover_off is not displayed (meaning the toggle is already On), logs that the toggle is already on and asserts False.

parameters.name: subject_text
parameters.description: Subject line text to enter in cover page subject field. Defaults to 'Test Fax'

functions.what_it_does: Enters subject text in the cover page subject field and clicks Send fax button. Sends subject_text to the subject input field using send_keys_to_element(). Then checks if send_fax_button is displayed, clicks it to submit the fax job, logs the action, and asserts True. If send_fax_button is not displayed, logs error and asserts False.

Validates the complete fax transmission status sequence by checking for all four status messages in order: Dialing, Ringing, Sending, and Fax Delivered!. For each status, checks if the corresponding locator is displayed using element_displayed(), logs success or failure, and asserts True/False. The fax_delivered_msg check uses a 180 second timeout to allow time for full transmission. This is the primary verification method for fax send tests and must return successfully for the test to pass.

functions.method_signature: def basic_validation_of_mobile_fax(self, fax_num = fax_number_to, to_name_="Recipient_01", phone_num=from_phone_number, name= "fax_test", subject_text = "Test Fax")

parameters.name: fax_num
parameters.description: Recipient fax number. Defaults to class constant fax_number_to

parameters.name: phone_num
parameters.description: Sender phone number. Defaults to class constant from_phone_number

parameters.name: subject_text
parameters.description: Cover page subject. Defaults to 'Test Fax'

functions.what_it_does: Executes the complete basic Mobile Fax workflow from start to finish. Calls navigate_to_mobile_fax() to open compose screen, logs the fax number being entered, calls enter_fax_number_to_add() with fax_num, logs the phone number being entered, calls enter_phone_number_and_name() with phone_num and name, calls click_on_files_cover_page_add_cover_button() to enable cover page, calls enter_subject() with subject_text which also clicks Send fax, calls validate_dialing_ringing_sending_fax_delivered_msg() to verify successful delivery, and logs completion. This is a composite workflow method that combines all steps for a basic fax send test.
functions.usage_example:
mf = MobileFax(appium_driver)
mf.basic_validation_of_mobile_fax(fax_num='2135312487', to_name_='John Doe', phone_num='8558250000', name='Test User', subject_text='Test Fax')

parameters.name: phone_num
parameters.description: Invalid phone number to test. Defaults to '1234567890'

functions.what_it_does: Validates that entering an invalid phone number format displays the correct error messages. Sends phone_num to the phone_number field using send_keys_to_element(). Finds the business_name element using driver.find_element() and clicks it to trigger validation. Checks if phone_num_error_msg ('Invalid Format') is displayed, logs success, then clicks files_and_cover_page_ID to navigate away. Checks if from_phone_num_error_msg ('Valid sender information is required to send a fax') is displayed and logs result. Asserts False if any expected error message is not displayed.

functions.method_signature: def validate_clear_all_fields(self, fax_num = fax_number_to, phone_num=from_phone_number, name= "fax_test", subject_text = "Test Fax")

parameters.name: fax_num
parameters.description: Recipient fax number. Defaults to class constant fax_number_to

parameters.name: phone_num
parameters.description: Sender phone number. Defaults to class constant from_phone_number

parameters.name: subject_text
parameters.description: Cover page subject. Defaults to 'Test Fax'

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the Clear all fields button correctly resets all compose form fields to empty state. Calls navigate_to_mobile_fax(), enter_fax_number_to_add(), enter_phone_number_and_name(), click_on_files_cover_page_add_cover_button(), and sends subject_text to subject field to populate all fields. Then checks if clear_all_fields button is displayed, clicks it, and verifies that fax_number field is displayed (indicating form reset) and to_add button is displayed (indicating empty state). Logs each step and asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

functions.method_signature: def navigate_to_cover_page(self, fax_num = fax_number_to, phone_num=from_phone_number, name= "fax_test", subject_text = "Test Fax")

parameters.name: fax_num
parameters.description: Recipient fax number. Defaults to class constant fax_number_to

parameters.name: phone_num
parameters.description: Sender phone number. Defaults to class constant from_phone_number

parameters.name: subject_text
parameters.description: Cover page subject. Defaults to 'Test Fax'

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Files and Cover Page tab with all recipient and sender fields populated and cover page enabled. Calls navigate_to_mobile_fax(), enter_fax_number_to_add() with fax_num, enter_phone_number_and_name() with phone_num and name, click_on_files_cover_page_add_cover_button() to enable cover page, and sends subject_text to subject field. This is a setup method for tests that need to start from the cover page configuration state.

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: Name of the file in the data/ folder. Defaults to 'Fax_pages.pdf'

functions.what_it_does: Constructs and returns the absolute file path for a test file in the framework's data/ folder. Uses os.path.join() with os.path.dirname(os.getcwd()) to go up one directory level from the current working directory and then appends 'data' and the provided file_name. Returns the complete path string that can be used with pyautogui.write() to enter file paths in Windows file picker dialogs.

parameters.name: locator
parameters.description: Appium locator tuple for the element to scroll to

parameters.name: max_swipes
parameters.description: Maximum number of swipe attempts. Defaults to 5

functions.what_it_does: Scrolls the HP Smart app screen vertically until the specified element becomes visible or max_swipes is reached. Loops up to max_swipes times, checking if the locator is displayed using element_displayed() with 2 second timeout. If found, returns True immediately. If not found, performs a vertical swipe up gesture using driver.swipe() with coordinates calculated from window size (start at 80% height, end at 30% height, centered horizontally, 600ms duration). Returns False if element is not found after all swipe attempts.

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: Name of the file in data/ folder to attach. Defaults to 'Fax_pages.pdf'

functions.what_it_does: Attaches a file from the data/ folder to the Mobile Fax. Calls scroll_until_visible() to scroll to add_files_photos button, clicks it to open Windows file picker. Waits for file_name_add field using wait_for_object(). Constructs file path using get_the_file_path(), logs the path, uses pyautogui.write() to type the path into the file picker, waits 2 seconds, presses Enter using pyautogui.press('enter'), waits 5 seconds for upload. Verifies upload success by checking if add_files_photos is displayed again with 30 second timeout. Logs success or failure and asserts False if upload fails.

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: Name of the file in data/ folder to attach twice. Defaults to 'Fax_pages.pdf'

functions.what_it_does: Uploads the same file twice to test the 50-page fax limit. Calls upload_file_to_fax() with file_name twice in succession. This is used to trigger the max_pages_error_msg by exceeding the page limit.

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: Name of the image file in data/ folder to import as scanned document. Defaults to 'Fax_page_png.png'

functions.what_it_does: Imports an image file through the scan-to-fax workflow instead of actually scanning. Checks if scan_file_button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it to open scan interface. Checks if scan_file_load is displayed, clicks import_link to open file picker instead of scanning. Waits for file_name_add field, constructs file path using get_the_file_path(), types it with pyautogui.write(), waits 2 seconds, presses Enter, waits 3 seconds. Checks if apply_button is displayed and clicks it. Checks if continue_fax_button is displayed and clicks it to return to compose screen. Verifies success by checking if add_files_photos is displayed with 50 second timeout. Logs each step and asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

Opens the scan interface from the Files and Cover Page tab. Checks if scan_file_button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it to open scan interface. Then checks if scan_file_load button is displayed in the scan interface and logs success or failure. Asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

Executes an actual scan operation in the scan-to-fax workflow. Checks if scan_button is displayed with 80 second timeout, clicks it to start scanning, logs the action. Waits for scan to complete and checks if continue_fax_button is displayed with 60 second timeout, clicks it to return to compose screen. Verifies success by checking if add_files_photos is displayed with 60 second timeout indicating the scanned document was attached. Logs each step and asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

Validates that the 50-page limit error notification appears and can be dismissed. Checks if max_pages_error_msg is displayed, logs the error message locator, checks if close_button is displayed, clicks it to dismiss the notification, waits 2 seconds. Logs each step and asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.
Clicks the Send fax button to submit the fax job. Checks if send_fax_button is displayed, clicks it, logs the action. Asserts False if button is not displayed.

Validates that a fax can be canceled during transmission and the cancel confirmation message appears. Checks for dialing, Ringing, and sending status messages in sequence to confirm transmission started. Then checks if cancel_fax_button is displayed, clicks it to cancel the fax, and checks if fax_cancel_error_msg ('Your fax was canceled.') is displayed. Logs each step and asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

Validates that all three action buttons appear in the fax success screen after delivery. Checks if delete_fax_button is displayed and logs result with assert True/False. Checks if edit_and_forward_button is displayed and logs result with assert True/False. Checks if fax_reports_button is displayed and logs result with assert True/False. This verifies the complete success screen UI is present after fax delivery.

parameters.name: fax_num
parameters.description: Invalid fax number to test validation

functions.what_it_does: Validates that entering an invalid fax number format displays the correct error messages. Sends fax_num to fax_number field using send_keys_to_element(). Clicks to_name_field to trigger validation. Checks if invalid_data error message is displayed and logs result with assert True/False. Checks if from_link_ID is displayed with 20 second timeout, clicks it to navigate to From tab. Checks if to_num_validation error message ('Valid recipient information is required to send a fax') is displayed and logs result with assert True.

parameters.name: fax_num
parameters.description: Invalid fax number to test. Defaults to class constant invalid_fax_number_to

functions.what_it_does: Complete workflow method for validating fax number format. Calls navigate_to_mobile_fax() to open compose screen, calls fax_to_validation() with fax_num to test validation, waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5).

parameters.name: subject_text
parameters.description: Subject text to enter before saving as draft

functions.what_it_does: Saves the current fax composition as a draft. Sends subject_text to subject field using send_keys_to_element(). Checks if send_fax_button is displayed and logs result with assert True/False. Checks if save_as_draft button is displayed, clicks it, logs the action with assert True/False. Checks if draft_saved confirmation message is displayed and logs result with assert True/False. Checks if save_draft_to label is displayed and logs result with assert True/False. Checks if draft_edit button is displayed and logs result with assert True.

Deletes a saved draft fax. Checks if draft_delete button is displayed, clicks it, logs the action with assert True/False. Checks if delete_pop_up confirmation dialog ('Are you sure?') is displayed, clicks the delete button to confirm deletion, logs the action.

functions.method_signature: def draft_save_delete(self, fax_num = fax_number_to, phone_num=from_phone_number, name= "fax_test", subject_text = "Test Fax")

parameters.name: fax_num
parameters.description: Recipient fax number. Defaults to class constant fax_number_to

parameters.name: phone_num
parameters.description: Sender phone number. Defaults to class constant from_phone_number

parameters.name: subject_text
parameters.description: Cover page subject. Defaults to 'Test Fax'

functions.what_it_does: Complete workflow method for testing draft save and delete functionality. Calls navigate_to_mobile_fax(), enter_fax_number_to_add() with fax_num, enter_phone_number_and_name() with phone_num and name, click_on_files_cover_page_add_cover_button() to enable cover page, save_as_a_draft() with subject_text to save the draft, delete_draft() to delete it, and waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5).

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: Name of the large file in data/ folder to test size limit. Defaults to 'Fax_pages.pdf'

functions.what_it_does: Uploads a file to test the 20 MB file size limitation. Calls scroll_until_visible() to scroll to add_files_photos button, clicks it to open Windows file picker. Waits for file_name_add field using wait_for_object(). Constructs file path using get_the_file_path(), logs the path, uses pyautogui.write() to type the path, waits 5 seconds, presses Enter using pyautogui.press('enter'), waits 10 seconds for upload attempt. This method is used to trigger the file size limit error notification.

Validates that the 20 MB file size limit error notification appears and can be dismissed. Checks if size_limitation_close button is displayed with 80 second timeout, logs the error notification message ('The file size exceeds the limit of 20 MB. Reduce the file size and try again.'), clicks the close button, logs the action. Then checks again if size_limitation_close is displayed with 20 second timeout (in case there are multiple close buttons) and clicks it. Asserts False if close button is not displayed.
Opens the Fax Settings screen from Mobile Fax. Checks if fax_settings button is displayed, clicks it, logs success. Asserts False if button is not displayed.
Opens the Contacts management screen from Fax Settings. Checks if Contacts option is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it, logs success. Asserts False if option is not displayed.
Opens the Add Contact form from Contacts management screen. Checks if add_contact button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it, logs success. Asserts False if button is not displayed.

Fills in the Add Contact form fields. Checks if Fax_Num field is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, sends contact['fax_number'] to it using send_keys_to_element(). Checks if Con_Name field is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, sends contact['contact_name'] to it. Logs error and asserts False if any field is not displayed.
Clicks the Save button in Add Contact or Add Cover Page forms. Checks if Save_button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it, logs success. Asserts False if button is not displayed.

parameters.name: contacts
parameters.description: List of contact dictionaries, each containing 'fax_number' and 'contact_name' keys

functions.what_it_does: Complete workflow method for saving multiple contacts. Calls navigate_to_mobile_fax() and click_fax_settings_and_verify() to open Fax Settings. Loops through each contact in the contacts list, calling click_contacts_and_verify(), click_add_contact_and_verify(), fax_num_con_num() with the contact dict, save_button_vali(), and logging success for each contact. Logs completion message after all contacts are saved.
functions.usage_example:
contacts = [{'fax_number': '2135312487', 'contact_name': 'John Doe'}, {'fax_number': '2135312488', 'contact_name': 'Jane Smith'}]
mf = MobileFax(appium_driver)
mf.save_contact_validation(contacts)

functions.what_it_does: Opens the Cover Pages management screen from Fax Settings. Checks if Cover_page option is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it, logs success. Asserts False if option is not displayed.

Opens the Add Cover Page form from Cover Pages management screen. Waits for Add_cov_Page button using wait_for_object() with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it, logs success. Logs error and asserts False if button is not found.

parameters.name: cover_name
parameters.description: Template name for the cover page

parameters.name: subject_name
parameters.description: Subject line for the cover page

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Message body text for the cover page

functions.what_it_does: Fills in the Add Cover Page form fields. Checks if Cover_page_name field is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, sends cover_name to it, logs success. Checks if Sub_name field is displayed, sends subject_name to it, logs success. Checks if Mess_age field is displayed, sends message to it, logs success. Calls save_button_vali() to click Save. Checks if Repl_ace button is displayed with 5 second timeout (appears if template name already exists), clicks it if present, logs action. Logs error and asserts False if any required field is not displayed.
functions.usage_example:
mf.Add_cov_page_button()
mf.add_new_cover_page(cover_name='Template1', subject_name='Urgent', message='Please review attached document')

parameters.name: cover_pages
parameters.description: List of cover page dictionaries, each containing 'cover_name', 'subject_name', and 'message' keys

functions.what_it_does: Complete workflow method for creating multiple cover page templates. Logs start message, calls navigate_to_mobile_fax() and click_fax_settings_and_verify() to open Fax Settings. Loops through each cover_page dict in cover_pages list, calling cover_page() to open Cover Pages screen, checking if Add_cov_Page button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicking it, calling add_new_cover_page() with the cover page data, clicking Save_button, and logging success for each template. Logs error if Add_cov_Page is not displayed. Logs completion message after all templates are created.
functions.usage_example:
cover_pages = [{'cover_name': 'Template1', 'subject_name': 'Urgent', 'message': 'Please review'}, {'cover_name': 'Template2', 'subject_name': 'FYI', 'message': 'For your information'}]
mf = MobileFax(appium_driver)
mf.cover_page_validation(cover_pages)

functions.method_signature: def send_fax_via_saved_contact_and_cover_page(self, contacts, cover_pages, subject_text="Test Fax", contact=None)

parameters.name: contacts
parameters.description: List of saved contacts (not used in current implementation)

parameters.name: cover_pages
parameters.description: List of saved cover pages (not used in current implementation)

parameters.name: subject_text
parameters.description: Subject text for the fax. Defaults to 'Test Fax'

parameters.name: contact
parameters.description: Specific contact to use (not used in current implementation)

functions.what_it_does: Sends a fax using a saved contact and saved cover page template. Calls navigate_to_mobile_fax(). Checks if fax_settings is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, uses pyautogui.click() with hardcoded coordinates (1855, 238) to click contact icon, logs success. Checks if Saved_contact_tab is displayed, clicks it, waits 5 seconds, uses pyautogui.click() with hardcoded coordinates (1636, 343) to click contact name. Logs error if Saved_contact_tab is not displayed. Checks if from_link_ID is displayed, clicks it, logs success. Logs error if not displayed. Calls enter_phone_number_and_name() to fill sender info. Calls click_on_files_cover_page_add_cover_button() to enable cover page. Checks if Cover_page_Template is displayed, clicks it, uses pyautogui.click() with hardcoded coordinates (1842, 302) to select template, waits 5 seconds. Logs error and asserts False if not displayed. Checks if Template_button is displayed, clicks it, logs success. Logs error and asserts False if not displayed. Calls click_the_send_fax_button() to send and validate_dialing_ringing_sending_fax_delivered_msg() to verify delivery. Logs completion message.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: MobileFax is a page object class for the HP Smart Windows app Mobile Fax feature and requires an active Appium WinAppDriver session passed via the appium_driver pytest fixture. It inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and uses Appium tuple locators exclusively (AppiumBy.NAME, AppiumBy.ACCESSIBILITY_ID, AppiumBy.XPATH). The class is instantiated with mf = MobileFax(appium_driver). The standard Mobile Fax workflow is: navigate_to_mobile_fax() → enter_fax_number_to_add() → enter_phone_number_and_name() → click_on_files_cover_page_add_cover_button() → enter_subject() → validate_dialing_ringing_sending_fax_delivered_msg(). The class provides composite workflow methods like basic_validation_of_mobile_fax() that combine all steps. File attachment uses pyautogui to interact with Windows file picker dialogs. The class includes methods for managing contacts and cover page templates in Fax Settings. Some methods use hardcoded pyautogui.click() coordinates which may be fragile across different screen resolutions. The validate_dialing_ringing_sending_fax_delivered_msg() method is the primary verification method and must return successfully for fax send tests to pass.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object class for HP Smart Windows app Print Documents feature automation. Contains PrintDocuments which inherits HPSmartBasicActions and provides all Appium tuple locators for Print Documents screen, file upload, print settings dropdowns (orientation, paper size, scaling, paper type, output quality, page range), More Settings dialog with advanced options (collation, paper tray, color mode, document copy count), printer management (add printer, printer selection), and print job validation. Provides complete workflow methods for uploading documents, configuring all print settings, accessing More Settings advanced options, adding printers via IP address, submitting print jobs, and validating print completion notifications.

classes.class_purpose: Page object class for automating the HP Smart Windows app Print Documents feature using Appium WinAppDriver. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions which provides base Appium interaction methods. Defines class-level Appium tuple locators for all Print Documents UI controls including file upload, print settings dropdowns (orientation, paper size, scaling, paper type, output quality, copies, page range), More Settings link and dialog with advanced options (collation, paper tray, color mode, document copy count), printer management (add printer via IP, printer selection), and print job submission and validation. Provides complete print workflow methods: navigate to Print Documents, upload file, configure basic settings, access More Settings for advanced options, add printers, submit print jobs, and validate print completion via toast notifications using uiautomation.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance from appium_driver pytest fixture for HP Smart Windows app automation

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PrintDocuments page object by calling the parent HPSmartBasicActions constructor with super().__init__(driver) and storing the driver reference as self.driver. All locators are defined as class-level attributes outside __init__ so no locator setup happens in the constructor. This class requires an active Appium WinAppDriver session passed via the appium_driver pytest fixture.

Navigates from HP Smart app home screen to the Print Documents screen. Waits for printer_online status using wait_for_object() with basic_wait_time timeout. Checks if print_documents tile is displayed using element_displayed() with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it using click_me(), logs success. Asserts False if print_documents tile is not displayed.

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: Name of the file in the data/ folder. Defaults to 'Fax_pages.pdf'

functions.docstring: These method useful to get the path of the file
functions.what_it_does: Constructs and returns the absolute file path for a test file in the framework's data/ folder. Uses os.path.join() with os.path.dirname(os.getcwd()) to go up one directory level from the current working directory and then appends 'data' and the provided file_name. Returns the complete path string that can be used with pyautogui.write() to enter file paths in Windows file picker dialogs.

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: Name of the file in data/ folder to upload for printing. Defaults to 'Fax_one_page.pdf'

functions.docstring:
Uploads a file to the print document section.
Parameters:
file name (str): The name of the file to be uploaded. Default is "Fax_one_page.pdf".
functions.what_it_does: Uploads a document file from the data/ folder to Print Documents for printing. Waits for file_name_add field using wait_for_object() with 20 second timeout. Constructs file path using get_the_file_path(), logs the path, uses pyautogui.write() to type the path into the Windows file picker, waits 2 seconds, presses Enter using pyautogui.press('enter'), waits 5 seconds for upload. Verifies upload success by checking if print_button is displayed with 30 second timeout. Logs success or failure and asserts False if upload fails.

Selects the print option check box type for printing.
Parameter:1
print option  checkbox (str): Basic options 'Delivery Mode',
Document options "media type", Paper Source, Booklet Printing
Advanced options 'Page Order', 'HP EasyColor',Borderless Printing
Parameter:2
check box dropdown options(str): example 'media type' option:'HP Photo Papers', 'Paper Source'='Tray'
functions.what_it_does: Selects a checkbox-based print option and its dropdown value in More Settings dialog. Constructs dynamic XPath locator using check_box_xpath format string with the print_option parameter value to find the checkbox. Checks if the checkbox is displayed with time_30 timeout, clicks it to expand the dropdown, logs action. Constructs dynamic XPath locator using drop_down_option_xpath format string with the drop_down parameter value. Checks if the dropdown option is displayed with time_30 timeout, clicks it to select, logs success. Asserts False if checkbox or dropdown option is not displayed.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of copies to print. Defaults to 5

functions.docstring:
Increases the page count for printing.
Parameters:
count (int): The number of copies to print. Default is 5.
functions.what_it_does: Increases the document copy count in More Settings dialog. Checks if document_copy_count_id input box is displayed with time_30 timeout. Constructs a PlusButton locator using AppiumBy.ACCESSIBILITY_ID. Loops count-1 times (since default is 1 copy), clicking the PlusButton each iteration and logging the click count. Asserts False if document_copy_count_id is not displayed.

Clicks the OK button in More Settings dialog to apply settings and close the dialog. Checks if name_ok button is displayed with time_30 timeout, clicks it, logs action. Asserts False if button is not displayed.

Clicks the Print button in More Settings screen and then the final Print button in the main Print Documents screen. Checks if print_button_id is displayed with time_30 timeout, clicks it, logs action. Then checks if print_button is displayed with time_20 timeout, clicks it, logs action. Asserts False if either button is not displayed.

Handles the Supported Document File Types popup that appears on first use of Print Documents. Checks if support_doc_file_pop is displayed with time_5 timeout, logs popup display. Checks if check_box ('Do not show this message again') is displayed with time_5 timeout, clicks it, logs action. Checks if ok_btn is displayed with time_5 timeout, clicks it to dismiss popup, logs action. Asserts False if any expected element is not displayed. If popup is not displayed, logs that it was not shown (normal for subsequent uses).

functions.docstring:
Adds a printer using the specified IP address.
Parameters:
ip_address (str): The IP address of the printer to add.
functions.what_it_does: Opens the Add Printer screen in HP Smart app. Checks if add_printer button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5), clicks the button, logs action. Checks if searching_for_printers message is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout to verify Add Printer screen opened, logs success or failure. Asserts False if add_printer button is not displayed or Add Printer screen does not open.

parameters.name: ip_address
parameters.description: IP address of the printer to add (e.g., '192.168.1.100')

functions.what_it_does: Enters printer IP address in Add Printer screen. Checks if printer_ip field is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it to focus, logs action. Uses pyautogui.write() to type the ip_address, waits 2 seconds, presses Enter using pyautogui.press('enter'), waits 5 seconds for printer discovery. Asserts False if printer_ip field is not displayed.

Clicks on the discovered online printer to add it. Checks if check_printer_status ('Online') is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it, logs action. Asserts False if Online status is not displayed.

Verifies that the printer driver information retrieval screen appears after clicking online printer. Checks if getting_printer_info message is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, logs success or failure. Asserts False if message is not displayed.

Opens Windows Settings Printers & Scanners page during printer setup. Checks if printers_scanners element is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, logs display, waits 5 seconds. Checks if vertical_scroll_down button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, calls scroll_to_elements() with vertical_scroll_down and printers_scanners to scroll into view, waits 5 seconds, clicks printers_scanners, logs action, waits 30 seconds for Settings to open. Asserts False if any expected element is not displayed.

Clicks Continue button in Add Printer workflow. Checks if continue_to_add button is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout, clicks it, logs action. Checks if finding_printer message is displayed with basic_wait_time timeout to verify printer search started, logs success or failure. Asserts False if continue_to_add button is not displayed or finding_printer message does not appear.

parameters.name: ip_address
parameters.description: IP address of the printer to check/add

functions.what_it_does: Checks if printer is already added to HP Smart app and adds it if not. Checks if printer_image is displayed with time_30 timeout. If displayed, logs that printer was already added, checks if previous_button is displayed with time_30 timeout, clicks it, waits 5 seconds, logs action, then calls click_add_printer(), add_printer_through_ip() with ip_address, and click_on_online_printer() to re-add. If printer_image is not displayed (printer not added), logs that printer was not added earlier and calls click_add_printer(), add_printer_through_ip() with ip_address, click_on_online_printer(), get_printer_info_after_clicking_online(), click_on_printer_scanner(), and click_on_continue_to_add_printer() to complete the add printer workflow. Asserts False if previous_button is not displayed when printer was already added.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for both notifications. Defaults to 300 seconds (5 minutes)

functions.docstring:
Waits up to `timeout` seconds for both 'Print in progress' and 'Print complete' notifications
to appear and be visible in sequence.
functions.what_it_does: Validates print job completion by checking for Windows toast notifications using uiautomation. Records start time using time.time(). Initializes found_progress and found_complete flags to False. Loops while elapsed time is less than timeout. In each iteration, gets desktop root control using auto.GetRootControl(). Searches for 'Print in progress' TextControl using desktop.Control() with Name and ControlType. If not found_progress and control Exists() and not IsOffscreen, logs success and sets found_progress to True. Searches for 'Print complete' TextControl. If found_progress and not found_complete and control Exists() and not IsOffscreen, logs success and sets found_complete to True. If both found_progress and found_complete are True, logs final success and returns True. Catches exceptions and logs warnings. Sleeps 0.1 seconds between iterations. After timeout, logs errors for any missing notifications and returns False.
functions.usage_example:
pd.click_print_button_in_print_doc()
result = pd.validate_print_toast_notifications_in_print_doc(timeout=300)
assert result, 'Print job did not complete successfully'

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the Print button in HP Smart Printing driver dialog using uiautomation. Waits 15 seconds using time.sleep(15). Uses auto.ButtonControl() with AutomationId 'PrintingToDialog.ui_ContentFrame.PrintButton' to find the print button. Checks if button Exists() with 1 second timeout and not IsOffscreen, logs success, calls Click() on the button, logs action. If button is not found or is offscreen, logs error and raises AssertionError. Catches exceptions and raises AssertionError with exception details.

functions.docstring: Click the close button on the print dialog.
functions.what_it_does: Clicks Print button and then closes the HP Smart Printing driver dialog. Checks if print_button_id is displayed, clicks it, logs action. Asserts False if not displayed. Waits 30 seconds using time.sleep(30). Uses auto.ButtonControl() with AutomationId 'PrintingToDialog.ui_ContentFrame.TitleBar.WindowCloseButton' to find the close button. Calls SetFocus() on the button. Loops up to 3 attempts (max_attempts=3). In each attempt, checks if button Exists() with 1 second timeout and not IsOffscreen, logs success, calls Click() on the button. If button no longer Exists() after click, logs success and breaks. Otherwise continues to next attempt. If button is not found, logs message and breaks. Catches exceptions and logs warnings. Logs error if any exception occurs during the entire process.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: PrintDocuments is a page object class for the HP Smart Windows app Print Documents feature and requires an active Appium WinAppDriver session passed via the appium_driver pytest fixture. It inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and uses Appium tuple locators (AppiumBy.NAME, AppiumBy.ACCESSIBILITY_ID, AppiumBy.XPATH, AppiumBy.ID, AppiumBy.CLASS_NAME). The class is instantiated with pd = PrintDocuments(appium_driver). The standard Print Documents workflow is: navigate_to_print_document() → supported_document_file_pop_up() (first use only) → upload_file_to_print_document() → [configure settings methods] → click_more_settings() (optional for advanced options) → [configure More Settings] → click_ms_ok_button() → click_print_button_in_print_doc() → validate_print_toast_notifications_in_print_doc(). File upload uses pyautogui to interact with Windows file picker dialogs. The class provides extensive settings configuration methods using dynamic XPath locators with format strings. The validate_print_toast_notifications_in_print_doc() method uses uiautomation (not Appium) to detect Windows toast notifications and is the primary verification method for print tests. The class includes comprehensive printer management methods for adding printers via IP address. Some methods like click_print_button_in_print_doc() and validate_print_toast_notifications_in_print_doc() use uiautomation directly instead of inherited Appium methods because they interact with Windows system UI elements outside the HP Smart app window.

MobileFax.cover_page_validation — Creates multiple cover page templates
PrintDocuments.navigate_to_print_document — Navigates to Print Documents screen from HP Smart home
PrintDocuments.click_more_settings — Opens More Settings advanced options dialog
PrintDocuments.validate_print_toast_notifications_in_print_doc — Validates print completion via Windows toast notifications
PrintDocuments.click_print_button_in_print_doc — Clicks Print button in HP Smart Printing driver dialog
PrintDocuments.check_the_printer_is_added_or_not — Checks if printer is added and adds if not
PrintDocuments.select_orientation — Selects Portrait or Landscape orientation
PrintDocuments.select_paper_size — Selects paper size (Letter, A4, Legal, etc.)

MobileFax.compose_fax — (AppiumBy.NAME, 'Compose Fax') — Opens fax composition form

commit_message: [NPI] Batch 4 analysis report | 5 files analyzed | 2 classes | 66 methods | 140 locators
